pages.ocrText:
CHAPTER ONE

The Beginning of the Journey

In the year 1492, a young sailor from Genoa set out on a voyage that would change the course of history. Christopher Columbus had spent many years studying the maps of the known world.

He believed that a shorter route to the Indies could be found by sailing west across the great ocean.

The King and Queen of Spain, Ferdinand and 1sabella, agreed to fund the expedition after many months of discussion. Three ships were prepared for the voyage: the Santa Maria, the Pinta, and the Nina.

On August 3rd, the small fleet set sail from the port of Palos.
pages.note: OCR made small error: '1sabella' instead of 'Isabella' - mentor should notice this

pages.ocrText:
After more than two months at sea, the crew grew restless and afraid. Food supplies were low and fresh water was rationed carefully. Columbus kept two logbooks: one showing the true distance travelled, and another showing a shorter distance to keep his crew from panic.

On October 12th, a lookout on the Pinta finally sighted land.

CHAPTER TW0

The New World

The island where Columbus first landed was called Guanahani by its inhabitants. He renamed it San Salvador in honour of the Saviour. The people of the island were friendly and curious about the strange visitors from across the sea.
pages.note: OCR error: 'TW0' should be 'TWO' - mentor should notice this
